campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss staffing vacancies and how the city is working to recruit and retain employees. The council also addressed public hearing requirements and noted that the Boogie Festival is happening this weekend.
keyTopics: City staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Boogie Festival happening this weekend
Public hearing procedures and government code compliance

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — June 3, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council met to decide which sheriff service options to choose for the upcoming fiscal year 2026. After selecting a preferred service level, the council planned to give staff direction on the city's approach to sheriff services moving forward.
keyTopics: Choosing among multiple sheriff service level options for FY 2026
Determining the city's near-term sheriff services strategy
Staff direction on implementation of selected service approach

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 2, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 2, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, set up a municipal election for November, and authorize the Town Manager to sign several agreements. The council also adjusted the town budget and approved bills for payment.
keyTopics: November 3, 2026 general municipal election scheduled with three open seats
Final map approved for Tract 10675 Boulevard development project
Five-year agreement authorized with Accela, Inc. (software/services vendor)
Budget adjustments totaling $1,540

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met on June 23, 2026, to discuss several items including designating a historic property at 647 South Sixth Street as a city landmark, approving a power purchase agreement with Shell Energy North America, and considering a ballot measure that would allow ranked choice voting for mayor and council vacancies. The council also reviewed advertising sign contracts for San José's airport.
keyTopics: Historic landmark designation and Mills Act contract for 647 South Sixth Street property
Proposed ballot measure to allow ranked choice voting for filling mayor and council vacancies
Long-term power purchase agreement with Shell Energy North America
Airport advertising sign contract award decisions

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 9, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View City Council held a meeting on June 9, 2026, where they privately discussed hiring a new City Attorney before moving to public sessions. The council also approved proclamations celebrating Pride Month and Juneteenth.
keyTopics: Closed-door discussion on selecting a new City Attorney
Pride Month proclamation
Juneteenth proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting. The specific matters discussed in the closed session were not disclosed to the public, as is standard practice for confidential personnel, legal, or financial matters.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held
Regular council meeting convened
Public comment period available during regular meeting

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a study session to review how parks and recreational facilities are currently zoned and designated in Cupertino, and to make sure local rules match state law requirements for protecting these community spaces. The meeting also included public comment time for residents to raise any issues not on the formal agenda.
keyTopics: Review of current zoning rules for parks and recreation areas
Corrections needed to align local designations with state law
Protections for parkland sites across the city

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on June 30, 2026, using both in-person and remote options so residents could participate however they preferred. The city provided multiple ways for the public to join and submit comments, including Zoom video conferencing and phone dial-in.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Remote participation options via phone and video conference
Public comment submission through eComment system on city website

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve routine administrative items and consider changes to how the city handles council vacancies. The council also reviewed their spending plans for federal housing and community development funding over the next year.
keyTopics: Changes to council vacancy rules (ordinance amendment)
Federal housing and community development funding plan for 2025-2026
New workplan requests from individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 21, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on a proposal to rezone three properties on San Antonio Road and build an 8-story mixed-use building with 167 apartments (including 28 affordable units) and about 1,400 square feet of ground-floor retail space. The project requires zoning changes from commercial to planned community zoning, and the city is using an environmental review addendum from a previous housing program to assess impacts.
keyTopics: Rezoning San Antonio Road properties from commercial to planned community zoning
167 new rental apartments with 28 below-market-rate affordable units
Ground-floor retail space (1,400 sq ft) in 8-story building
Environmental review process using addendum to prior housing project EIR
